info.title: Brainiac
info.description: GitOps for organizational AI knowledge — capture, govern, and serve an organization's memory. Every read is RLS-scoped to the caller: an agent can never retrieve what its operator cannot.

get.description: Liveness probe; unauthenticated.

get.summary:
The document itself. Open (like `/health`) — a client needs the contract
before it has a token, and the spec describes shapes, never data.
get.description: This OpenAPI document, derived from the handlers.
Governance health counters under the caller's RLS view: memories by status, review backlog, graph size, ingest queue depth.
The dashboard payload: status totals, weekly captured/promoted flow, kind×team volumes, top canonical themes, review latency, contradiction mix, queue depth.

get.summary:
Reverse-chronological feed of governance actions: promotion reviews
(human and policy) and contradiction resolutions. Reuses the reviewer /
resolved-by columns both tables already carry; rows resolve under the
caller's RLS transaction so members see their org slice only.
get.description: Reverse-chronological feed of governance actions: promotion reviews (human and policy) and contradiction resolutions.
The entity graph: canonical hubs, team-scoped surface entities, and evidence edges (bounded at 2000/2000/5000 rows).

get.summary:
L2/L3: one canonical entity's neighborhood — surface forms per team,
1-hop evidence edges (content RLS-scoped), neighbor canonicals reachable
through those edges, and the anchored memories the caller may read.
get.description: Cortex map L2/L3: one canonical entity's neighborhood — surface forms per team, 1-hop evidence edges, neighbor canonicals, and readable anchored memories.

get.summary:
L0/L1: team lobes with volumes, top canonical hubs with team spread, and
team-pair binding strength (shared canonicals). Bounded by construction.
get.description: Cortex map L0/L1: team lobes with volumes, the top 60 canonical hubs with their team spread, and team-pair binding strength.
Browse the memory archive, optionally as-of an instant (time travel over the validity windows). Returns the filtered total alongside the page.

parameters.name: as_of
parameters.description: RFC3339 instant: return rows VALID then, including since-deprecated ones

post.description: Ingest raw content as a source and enqueue the extraction pipeline (async).

get.summary:
The re-verification queue: live candidate/canonical memories whose
validity window closes within the horizon, oldest boundary first.
get.description: The re-verification queue: live candidate/canonical memories whose validity window closes within the horizon, oldest boundary first.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Horizon in days (default 30, clamped 0..3650; 0 = only already-expired)

post.description: Hybrid (vector + lexical + graph) retrieval under the caller's RLS scope.

get.description: One memory with its provenance, anchored entities, promotion history, and supersession lineage (signed chain depth: negative = predecessors).

404.description: Memory not found (or invisible under RLS)

post.summary:
Re-verification is a governance act: like promotion review, it requires
a maintainer of the owning team (org-wide memories: any org principal
with write scope).
post.description: Re-verify a memory: extend its validity window from now and close any open feedback claims against it. Requires a maintainer of the owning team.

requestBody.description: Optional validity budget; body may be omitted entirely

403.description: Caller is not a maintainer of the owning team

404.description: Memory not found, or superseded (supersessions are final)

get.summary:
Org directory for the key-mint picker (admin scope — it feeds token
management). Users/teams carry no RLS; org scoping is explicit.
get.description: Org directory for the key-mint picker: every user in the caller's org with their team memberships and roles. Requires admin scope.

get.summary: Recent pipeline runs — the worker's own audit trail, org-scoped by RLS.
get.description: Recent pipeline runs — the worker's own audit trail, newest first, org-scoped by RLS.
Dead-lettered jobs, most recent first (default limit 50).

parameters.name: queue
parameters.description: Queue name; defaults to the ingest queue.

post.description: Move a dead-lettered job back onto its queue for another attempt.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Archive row id of the dead-lettered job

404.description: Job is not in the dead-letter archive

get.description: Operator view of one queue: depth, in-flight, retry histogram, archive tallies.

parameters.name: queue
parameters.description: Queue name; defaults to the ingest queue.

get.description: The contradiction queue: oldest first (SLA aging), plus a status histogram that powers the queue's tabs.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: open | resolved_supersede | resolved_coexist | dismissed | all (default open)

parameters.name: min_age_hours
parameters.description: Only rows at least this many hours old

post.description: Resolve an open contradiction by supersede (deprecates the loser), coexist, or dismiss. Supersede requires a maintainer of the losing memory's team.

400.description: Unknown resolution, or supersede without a valid winner_memory_id

403.description: Supersede requires a maintainer of the losing memory's team

404.description: Contradiction not found / not open, or the losing memory is not visible

get.summary:
Memories carrying unresolved `wrong` / `outdated` claims from the agents
and operators who were served them — most-disputed first. This is where
MCP memory_feedback verdicts land for a human.
get.description: Memories carrying unresolved wrong/outdated claims from the agents and operators served them, most-disputed first.

post.summary:
Answer the open claims against a memory. The three answers are the three
things a maintainer can actually mean:
  reverified — checked it, still true → extend its validity window
  deprecated — the reporters are right → end it now, drop it from retrieval
  dismissed  — the reports are noise → leave the memory as-is
Whichever is chosen, every open claim on that memory closes with it.
post.description: Answer every open feedback claim against a memory: reverified (extend validity), deprecated (end it now), or dismissed (memory stands).

403.description: Caller is not a maintainer of the owning team

404.description: Memory not found (or invisible under RLS)

get.description: Promotions awaiting human review (oldest first, max 100).

post.description: Approve a pending promotion: moves the memory to the promotion's target status. Requires a maintainer of the owning team.

403.description: Caller is not a maintainer of the owning team

404.description: Promotion not found or no longer pending

post.description: Reject a pending promotion: the memory is marked rejected. Requires a maintainer of the owning team.

403.description: Caller is not a maintainer of the owning team

404.description: Promotion not found or no longer pending

get.summary:
Recent sources with their pipeline rollup — the monitor's feed. Queue
state joins outside RLS (queue schema is org-blind); org membership is
proven by the RLS read of `sources` itself.
get.description: Recent ingest sources with their pipeline rollup (memories, promoted, pending review) and derived queue status.

get.summary:
GET /v1/sources/{id} — what happened to an async memory_add. Closes the
loop on the 202: the source row (RLS-scoped), the queue job state, and
what the pipeline produced from it.
get.description: Ingestion status of a source: queue job state plus what the pipeline produced.

404.description: Source not found (or invisible under RLS — no oracle)

get.summary: GET /v1/tokens — list the org's tokens (metadata only, never secrets).
get.description: List the org's API tokens (metadata only; secrets are never retrievable).

post.description: Mint an API token; the plaintext secret is returned once and never again.

400.description: Empty name or scopes outside read|write|admin

post.summary:
Blast radius: exactly what a key minted for this principal could read,
computed by opening a transaction AS that principal — the same RLS path
the runtime uses, so the preview can't drift from enforcement.
post.description: Blast-radius preview for a key minted for a given user: what that principal could read, computed under their own RLS. Requires admin scope.

404.description: User not found in this org

post.description: Revoke a token immediately; subsequent requests with it are 401.

404.description: Token not found or already revoked

AnchorRef.description: A canonical entity anchoring a hit (id + name).

ArchivedCounts.description: Archive tallies, kept nested under `archived` (not flattened).

AttemptsBucket.description: One bucket of the retry-attempt histogram over the ready jobs.

AuditEvent.description:
One governance action. `kind` is `promotion_review` |
`contradiction_resolution`; `memory_b` is only set for contradictions.

actor_id.description: Null for policy (auto) decisions — no human actor.

CanonicalEdge.description:
A 1-hop edge touching a surface form; `evidence` is null when the backing
memory is invisible to the caller.

ChainLink.description:
One link of the supersession lineage. `depth` is **signed**: negative for
predecessors (walking back), positive for successors (walking forward).

ContradictionMemoryRef.description:
One side of a contradiction. `content` is null when the memory is not
visible to the caller under RLS (the row itself is org-scoped metadata).

counts.description: Status histogram over ALL contradictions — unaffected by the filters.

suggested_resolution.description:
`contradictions.resolution_note` — the detector's suggestion, or the
resolver's note once answered.

user_id.description: Principal the token acts as; defaults to the caller.

CreatedTokenResponse.description:
The mint response. `token` is the plaintext secret and is the only place
it will ever appear — everything else is retrievable from `GET /v1/tokens`.

token.description: Shown exactly once — never retrievable again.

DeadLetterEntry.description:
One dead-lettered job. `payload` is the opaque job JSON, passed through
verbatim (its shape belongs to the pipeline, not to this API).

ErrorResponse.description:
The JSON error body. Documented once in the OpenAPI spec and returned by
every error path.

error.description:
Human-readable message. Specific for business errors (400/401/403/404);
a generic `"internal error"` for 5xx faults (detail is logged, not sent).

ExpiringMemory.description:
A memory in the re-verification horizon. `days_left` is computed per row
against `now()` and is negative once the boundary has passed.

FeedbackClaims.description: Open claim counts against one memory.

notes.description: Reporter notes on the open claims (most recent first, capped).

oldest_claim_secs.description: Age of the OLDEST open claim — how long the dispute has stood.

GraphEdge.description:
An evidence edge. `evidence` is null when the backing memory is hidden by
RLS — the edge itself stays visible (it's org metadata).

canonical_id.description: Null when the entity has not been linked to a canonical hub yet.

HealthResponse.description: Liveness probe body — `{"status":"ok"}` and nothing else.

MemoryAcceptedResponse.description:
The 202 receipt: the source row that was written and the queue job that
will extract memories from it. Poll `GET /v1/sources/{id}` with `source_id`.

MemoryPromotion.description: A promotion attempt on this memory. Timestamps are RFC3339 strings here.

MemoryProvenance.description:
The provenance record behind a memory. The whole object is **null** (not
omitted) when the memory has no provenance row.

MemoryRow.description:
The archive's memory row. NOTE the timestamps here are **RFC3339
strings**, not `DateTime` — this handler stringifies them (unlike the
contradiction/audit/expiring payloads, which pass `DateTime` through).
`created_at` is non-null in practice but is read as an optional column, so
it stays `Option<String>` to keep the emitted shape identical.

teams.description:
Raw `json_agg` output — an array of `{id, name, role}` (empty array,
never null, when the user is on no team).

OrgUserTeam.description:
Documentation-only mirror of one element of `OrgUser::teams`. The value is
produced by Postgres `json_agg(json_build_object('id','name','role'))` and
is forwarded verbatim as `serde_json::Value` so the bytes cannot drift;
this struct exists purely to give the schema its real shape.

OverviewCanonical.description:
A canonical hub with its team spread. `teams` is the DISTINCT team count;
`team_ids` is the set itself.

PipelineRunRow.description:
One worker run. `started_at` is an RFC3339 **string**; `duration_secs`
measures against `now()` while the run is still open.

PromotionMemory.description:
Memory body of a pending promotion. `None` on the parent ⇒ serialized as
`null` (RLS-invisible memory) — never omitted.

PromotionProvenance.description: Provenance of a pending promotion; `null` alongside an invisible memory.

QueueDepth.description: Ingest queue depth — shared by `/v1/analytics` and the observatory.

RequeueResponse.description: Confirmation of a requeue; `requeued` is always `true` (a miss 404s).

winner_memory_id.description: Required for supersede: the memory that wins.

days.description: For `reverified`: the new validity budget (defaults to the kind TTL).

ResolveFeedbackResponse.description:
`valid_to` is null for `deprecated`/`dismissed` (only `reverified` moves
the boundary) — null, never absent.

days.description: New validity budget from now; defaults to the kind's standard TTL.
The budget actually applied, after clamping.

valid_to.description: The new validity boundary (never null on success).

ReviewDecisionResponse.description:
Outcome of an approve/reject: the promotion, the memory it moved, and the
status that memory now carries.

memory_status.description: The memory's status after the decision.

RevokeResponse.description: Confirmation of a revoke; `revoked` is always `true` (a no-op revoke 404s).

kinds.description: Memory kinds to keep (fact|decision|pattern|pitfall|howto); omit for all.

anchors.description:
Canonical entities anchoring this hit; for via_graph hits, the bridge it
surfaced through. Empty when the memory has no canonical-linked entities.

SourceInfo.description: The source row itself, as the caller's RLS scope sees it.

SourceJob.description:
Queue state of the ingest job. `null` on the parent once the job has aged
out of both `queue.jobs` and `queue.archive`.

outcome.description: Archive outcome (`ok` / failure reason); always null while queued.

SourceResults.description: What the pipeline produced from this source (nested, not flattened).

SourceRow.description:
A source with its pipeline rollup. `created_at` is an RFC3339 **string**
here (this handler stringifies it). `status` is derived from the queue
job + memory count: queued | retrying | processed | failed | unknown.

attempts.description: Queue delivery attempts; null when no queue job is known.

StatusCount.description:
`{status, count}` — the shape every status histogram in this module emits
(memories-by-status, contradiction tabs).

SurfaceForm.description:
A team-scoped surface form linked into the canonical hub, with the link's
confidence and resolution method.

TeamLink.description: Binding strength between two team lobes = canonicals both teams link into.

TeamLobe.description: A team lobe: the team plus its memory/entity volumes.

teams.description: Team names, not ids — this is the human-facing picker preview.

TokenSummary.description: One token as the listing exposes it — metadata only, never the secret.

TokenVisibility.description: What a key minted for this principal could read, by visibility tier.

WeeklyPoint.description:
One point of a weekly series; `week` is an ISO label (`IYYY-Www`) so the
captured/promoted series stay joinable client-side.

bearer.description: Env-map token (unrestricted) or a managed `brk_…` key carrying the read/write/admin scope the route requires.

tags.name: memories
tags.description: Retrieval, capture, and the memory ledger.

tags.name: reviews
tags.description: Governance queues: promotions, contradictions, disputed memories, and the audit trail.

tags.name: graph
tags.description: The canonical entity graph and its cortex map.

tags.name: analytics
tags.description: Governance health counters and the observatory.

tags.name: queue
tags.description: Job queue health and dead-letter recovery (admin).

tags.name: tokens
tags.description: Managed API keys and their blast radius (admin).
